name: ce-notes
currentState: ce-notes is a single Rust crate (edition 2024) implementing local-first, E2E-encrypted CRDT notes as a CE app over the ce-rs/ce-coord SDK, plus a CLI binary. Architecture is clean and genuinely layered: crypto.rs (XChaCha20-Poly1305 space key + X25519 sealed-box key wrapping, Ed25519->X25519 derivation), notedoc.rs (yrs/Yjs text CRDT behind a NoteDoc trait), mergelog.rs (one writer-log + N reader-logs per space, set-union of commutative ops over ce-coord), store.rs (at-rest sealed persistence), model.rs (SpaceMeta/MemberEntry/Role/NoteHeader/Folder/AttachmentRef/NoteOp/Invite), and notes.rs (the Notes/Space API). What genuinely works and is well-tested: the crypto envelope, key wrap/unwrap, deterministic X25519-from-NodeId derivation, CRDT convergence/idempotence, sealed at-rest storage, and the invite/import (ce-cap grant + wrapped-key handoff) path. There are NO todo!/unimplemented!/panic! and no unwrap/expect in production paths (all unwraps are in tests). Relative to Google Keep, however, the actual note model is just title + markdown body + folders + attachments: there is no search, no labels/tags, no pin/archive, no color, no checklists, and no reminders. Key rotation/revoke is documented-but-unimplemented, reader-role write-protection is not enforced app-side, the index is an unbounded append-only JSON-in-Y.Text log, and cross-device realtime sync relies on a 250ms poll with the applied/drained high-water marks never persisted.
plan: Fix the text-CRDT indexing bug: make notedoc::diff/set_text operate in UTF-16 code units (yrs's index space) instead of char scalars, and add a property test with emoji/non-BMP strings asserting convergence and exact round-trip. This is a silent data-corruption bug and the highest-leverage correctness fix.

Add the Keep-defining note attributes to NoteHeader and the model: pinned, archived, color, and a labels Vec<LabelId> plus a Label type; update notes() to pin-sort, hide archived, and add list-by-label/list-archived/list-trash + restore. Expose CLI subcommands (pin/unpin, archive/unarchive, color, label add/rm, trash ls/restore).

Implement structured checklists: a checklist note variant backed by a Y.Array of {id, text, checked, order} items with CRDT-safe add/check/reorder, distinct from the freeform markdown body, plus CLI commands (check/uncheck/add-item).

Add local full-text search: maintain an in-memory (and optionally on-disk sealed) inverted index over decrypted titles+bodies+labels, with a `ce-notes search <space> <query>` command; rebuild it in rebuild_index_state and update on set_note_text.
Enforce roles app-side: gate create_note/set_note_text/delete_note/create_folder behind meta.can_write(self.node_id) and return a typed error for readers; add failure-path tests.

Implement key rotation / revoke: Space::revoke(member) -> bump key_epoch, generate a new space key, re-wrap to every remaining active member, persist, and seal new ops under the new epoch; add an integration test that a revoked member cannot decrypt post-rotation ops while remaining members can.

Compact the index: replace the append-only JSON-in-Y.Text log with a structured CRDT (Y.Map per id, or periodic snapshot+truncate) so per-edit cost is O(1) and storage is bounded; add a growth/compaction test.
Persist and use the applied/drained high-water marks (wire Store::save_applied/load_applied into MergeSet open/pump) so reopen does not re-drain the whole history; add a resume-after-restart test.

Add bounds/limits and DoS guards: max body size, max attachment size with streaming/chunked encryption instead of whole-file read-into-RAM, max members, and a max decoded size for Invite/op deserialization; tests for over-limit rejection.
Replace the silent decrypt-failure swallow in apply_incoming with structured logging/metrics (tracing::warn with op.doc_id/epoch) distinguishing expected cross-epoch skips from unexpected failures.

Add reminders: a reminder field on NoteHeader (time-based first), persisted in the index CRDT, plus a `ce-notes reminders` listing of due/upcoming notes (notification delivery can be a later integration).

Add a real two-node e2e test (spawn two ce nodes, A and B, share via invite, assert cross-device convergence and offline-merge) wired into CI behind the existing CE_BIN gate, since live_node.rs currently cannot assert convergence.

Make the lib.rs doctest executable (drop no_run where feasible or add a compile-checked example under examples/) and add a CONTRIBUTING/threat-model doc; correct store.rs docs to match actual on-disk files.

featureGaps.notes: Google Keep's core UX is instant search over note text, labels, and types. There is no search command or Space method at all; notes() only lists headers sorted by updated_at. Need an index over decrypted body text (local-only, since mesh is ciphertext).

Keep is organized by colored labels, not just folders. model.rs has only Folder (single-parent tree) and NoteHeader has folder_id but no labels. Add a labels Vec<LabelId> to NoteHeader + a Label type + list-by-label filtering.
Keep pins notes to the top and archives them out of the main list. NoteHeader has no pinned/archived flags; notes() cannot pin-sort or hide archived. Add bool fields + sort/filter.

featureGaps.feature: Checklists (structured list items with checked state)
featureGaps.notes: A defining Keep feature. Body is a single freeform markdown Y.Text; there is no structured checklist model, no per-item checkbox state, no reordering. Needs a list-item CRDT (Y.Array of items) or a structured body type.
Keep notifies you at a time/place. No reminder field, no scheduling, no notification path anywhere in the model or CLI.
Keep color-codes notes. NoteHeader has no color field; trivially missing.

Documented as unimplemented (Space::revoke does not exist). MemberEntry has a revoked tombstone and SpaceMeta.key_epoch exists, but nothing rotates the space key or re-wraps to remaining members, so revoking a member does not actually cut off their access to future content.

Role::Reader exists and abilities() attenuates, but Space::set_note_text/delete_note/create_note never check meta.can_write(self). A local Reader can still publish write-ops to its writer-log; only a remote host enforcing the cap would reject them, and the app does not gate locally.

Keep supports inline images and basic formatting. Bodies are plain markdown text; attachments are not linked into the body structurally (linkage is a freeform marker line, per the code comment in attach()).
delete_note is a tombstone (good, recoverable in principle) but there is no command to list trashed notes, restore, or empty trash; deleted notes simply vanish from notes().
Keep shows collaborators; CRDT substrate could support presence but none is implemented.
Only a CLI exists; README notes the TUI and web client are unbuilt. Keep is primarily GUI/mobile.

robustnessIssues.issue: set_text diff computes prefix/suffix/removed lengths in Unicode char (scalar) units, but yrs Text indexes by UTF-16 code units. For any note containing non-BMP characters (emoji, many CJK supplementary, etc.) the remove_range/insert offsets are wrong, corrupting the CRDT delta and the converged text. The code comment acknowledges this but ships it.

The per-space index is an append-only newline-delimited JSON log stored inside a single Y.Text; every header upsert (every title edit, delete, folder change) appends a full JSON line and rebuild_index_state re-parses the entire text on every change. This grows unbounded and is O(n) per edit and O(n) memory — a DoS/bloat vector for large or long-lived notebooks. No compaction.

applied/drained high-water marks are never persisted. Store::save_applied/load_applied exist but notes.rs never calls them; MergeSet.drained always starts at 0 on open. On every reopen the app re-drains and re-applies the entire op history (decrypt + CRDT apply for every op ever published). Correct for LWW index but wastefully O(history) and silently divergent from the documented store layout.

No size/length limits anywhere: note bodies, attachment file size (std::fs::read of an arbitrary path into memory), index log, and number of members are all unbounded. A large attachment is read fully into RAM and sealed in one buffer; a malicious/huge invite or op is decoded without limits.
robustnessIssues.location: src/core/notes.rs:391-413 (attach reads whole file), 549-557 (apply_index_update), model.rs:186-194 (Invite::decode serde_json unbounded)

robustnessIssues.issue: Reader role is not enforced on the write path: set_note_text/create_note/delete_note do not consult meta.can_write(self.node_id). A member holding only Role::Reader can still author and publish write-ops locally.
robustnessIssues.location: src/core/notes.rs:306-365 (create/set/delete), model.rs:98-100 (can_write defined but unused on writes)

robustnessIssues.issue: apply_incoming silently swallows any op whose decryption fails (returns Ok(())). This is intended for cross-epoch ops, but it also masks genuinely corrupt/forged ops and key-rotation bugs with no logging/metric, making divergence undiagnosable.

store_clone() uses self.store.root().parent().unwrap_or(root) to recover the data_dir; if root has no parent (root path edge case) it silently re-opens the store at the wrong directory rather than erroring.

x25519_public_from_node_id can fail for NodeIds that are not valid Ed25519 points; invite() will then error, but there is no validation that the target is a real, point-on-curve NodeId before mutating membership/persisting — partial state could be written if a later step fails (no transactionality across save_meta + add_peer).
robustnessIssues.location: src/core/notes.rs:436-496 (invite mutates meta/persists then adds peer)

robustnessIssues.issue: now_secs() and note id construction format!("{:016x}{}", now_secs(), random_hex(8)) rely on wall-clock for ordering; clock skew/backwards time produces mis-ordered ids, and two notes created in the same second differ only by 8 random bytes (fine) but updated_at ties are broken only by note_id string compare.

testGaps.area: Multi-device convergence over a real mesh (two nodes, A writes / B reads / both edit offline then merge)
live_node.rs explicitly does NOT assert cross-device convergence (single node, two coord handles share one inbox ring); the actual peer-sync path is untested in CI
Non-BMP / emoji / multi-byte text through set_text -> CRDT delta -> converge (would catch the UTF-16 vs char indexing bug)
Index growth / compaction behavior and rebuild correctness after many upserts and deletes of the same note
Reader-role write rejection (currently no enforcement, so no test could pass) and Owner-only invite/revoke authorization
Key rotation / revoke round-trip: after rotation, revoked member cannot decrypt new ops, remaining members can
Concurrent local writers: many tokio tasks calling set_note_text/pump_once on one Space simultaneously (mutex ordering, no deadlock, no lost ops)
Attachment fetch when the cached plaintext is corrupt or size-mismatched, and when get_object returns tampered ciphertext
applied/drained persistence and resume-after-restart (currently the code path is dead, so untestable as written)
Invite tamper: a grant_token or wrapped_key altered in transit must be rejected on import

docGaps: README 'Status' honestly lists stubs (revoke, checkpointing, TUI, SSE, attachment linkage) but does NOT disclose the UTF-16/char indexing correctness risk or that reader-role is unenforced app-side.
No architecture/design doc in-repo (README references 'design doc §5-6' and milestones M1-M5 that are not present in the crate); the referenced spec is external/missing.
No rustdoc doctests are executable: the lib.rs example is no_run, so the public API surface is never compile-checked against doc drift.
No CONTRIBUTING or threat-model doc spelling out the confidentiality boundary, what the mesh sees, and the documented revocation/forward-secrecy tradeoff beyond a single README paragraph.
Store layout doc in store.rs mentions applied.json as part of the format, but the code never writes it — docs describe an unimplemented behavior.
No examples/ directory; the only runnable demos are the two-device shell/PowerShell scripts, which require external running nodes and are not covered by CI.

name: ce-chat
currentState: ce-chat is a Vite + TypeScript browser SPA (no framework, hand-rolled DOM helpers) that maps each chat channel/DM to a CE mesh pubsub topic and rides the @ce-net/sdk `CeClient.mesh` (subscribe/publish + one SSE `streamMessages` stream). The architecture is clean and genuinely decoupled: a narrow `MeshLike` port lets all core logic (protocol envelopes, topic mapping, dedupe store, presence TTL, error mapping, ChatService data path) be unit-tested against an in-memory fake mesh with no node. What truly works: public channels, capability-gated private channels (subscribe 403 -> friendly grant prompt), order-independent DMs derived from sorted node ids, optimistic send with echo-confirm/dedupe, heartbeat-driven presence with TTL aging, a 500-message ring cap per channel, and a polished UI (sidebar, roster, composer, modals, skeleton/offline states, linkify with no innerHTML injection). There are no `todo!()`/`unimplemented!()` and no mock data in the data path — only the test fake. Real gaps: presence only heartbeats into the *active* channel (peers in background channels never see you), no message persistence/history backfill (a fresh join shows an empty channel), and the entire feature set is "send/receive plain text + presence" — no threads, reactions, mentions, typing indicators, read receipts, or editing/deletion.
plan: Fix the dedupe-spoofing hazard: key the store and all dedupe on the tuple (from, id) instead of bare id, and on receive namespace the stored key by frame.from so a hostile/duplicate id from one peer cannot suppress another author's message or a victim's own send. Add a regression test.

Add message history backfill: define a `history-request`/`history-response` envelope (or use node durable storage if the SDK exposes it), so a node joining a channel asks peers for the last N messages and renders scrollback instead of an empty pane. This is the single biggest gap vs Slack/Chat.

Fan presence heartbeats out to ALL joined channels (not just app.active) on the heartbeat timer, so members stay online across background channels; add a test that a peer subscribed to two channels stays online in both.

Add automatic SSE stream reconnect with exponential backoff + jitter inside ChatService (cap, abortable), surfacing a transient 'reconnecting' state and auto-recovering live updates without a full boot()/reload; test recovery after an injected drop.
Persist the joined-channel list and DMs to localStorage and rehydrate on boot (instead of only the 3 hardcoded DEFAULT_CHANNELS), and persist per-channel composer drafts; test rehydration.

Implement reactions: add a `react` envelope ({targetId, emoji, op}), aggregate reaction counts in the store keyed by target message, and render a reaction bar + picker. Cover add/remove/dedupe in tests.
Implement threads: add an optional `replyTo` field to ChatMsg, group replies, and add a thread side-pane; keep root-channel rendering unchanged when replyTo is absent.

Implement @mentions + unread/notification model: parse @name/@nodeId tokens in linkify, highlight mentions of self, and track per-channel unread counts + a last-read marker rendered as badges in the sidebar.
Add typing indicators via a throttled `typing` envelope (rate-limited, TTL'd) and a transient roster/composer overlay; ensure it never persists to the message log.

Harden input validation and DoS bounds: length-cap the envelope `name` field in parseEnvelope, add per-sender inbound rate limiting, debounce/batch renderStream on bursts (rAF coalescing), and cap deeply nested/huge JSON before parse.

Replace the UI-only `failed` Set with a first-class message state in StoredMessage (status: pending|sent|failed) owned by the store/service, with a retry action that re-publishes; test the publish-failure path end to end.
Remove the non-null `app!`/DOM `!` assertions in main.ts by introducing guarded accessors that no-op when app is null or a node is missing, eliminating the race-throw on reconnect/reload.
Make the node URL configurable (localStorage/env override of DEFAULT_NODE_URL) so the app can target a remote or alternate-port node without editing source.

Add tests for the currently-untested layers: dom.ts (linkify boundaries + injection safety), sdk-adapter.ts (mesh frame normalization, safeUtf8 on invalid bytes), and a real two-node e2e harness behind an opt-in flag; add property tests for store ordering/eviction invariants.
Write ARCHITECTURE.md (MeshLike port, reducer/dedupe model, protocol versioning/compat policy) and a README Limitations section enumerating unsupported features and surprising behaviors.

featureGaps.notes: A newly-joined member sees an empty channel — pubsub only delivers live frames. There is no archive, no `since` query, no peer-served history. Real Slack/Chat always shows scrollback. Needs a history-request envelope answered by peers, or node-side durable storage.
ChatMsg has no parentId/threadId. No threaded view. Slack/Chat threads are core. Add a `replyTo` field to the envelope and a thread pane.
No reaction envelope type, no per-message reaction aggregation in the store. Add a `react` envelope ({targetId, emoji, op:add/remove}) and reducer state.
No mention parsing, no highlight, no unread/notification badge. linkify only handles URLs. Add @-token parsing keyed on node id/name and an unread-count model.
Listed in the reference set but absent. Needs a throttled `typing` heartbeat envelope and a transient roster overlay.
No per-channel unread tracking, no last-read marker, no receipt envelope. Sidebar shows online counts but never unread badges.
Envelopes are immutable; no edit/tombstone type. Real products allow edit/delete with an edited marker.
main.ts heartbeats only into app.active, so members of non-active channels age out and appear offline to peers even though the node is subscribed. Heartbeat should fan out to all joined channels.
Joined channels beyond the 3 hardcoded DEFAULT_CHANNELS are lost on reload — no localStorage of the channel list. Custom channels/DMs vanish on refresh.
Text-only. No CE blob integration despite the SDK likely exposing blobs. Slack/Chat support uploads + previews (formatBytes exists but is unused, hinting this was scoped out).
Only bare-URL linkify. No bold/italic/code-fence rendering, no emoji shortcodes.
No message search across channels.
service.leave() keeps the node subscription (SDK has no unsubscribe); a node accumulates topic subscriptions unboundedly over a long session.

robustnessIssues.issue: Many non-null assertions on `app!` and DOM queries in render/handlers (`const a = app!`, `service.joined().find(...)!`, `service.state(a.active)!`). If a render races a teardown/reconnect (boot() reassigns app, location.reload), these throw uncaught. App-layer convention bans unwrap in prod paths; these are the TS equivalent.

No rate limiting / flood control on inbound frames or the store: a malicious or buggy peer can publish unlimited messages; each unique id is stored (ring-capped at 500 per channel, fine) but onMessages fires a full renderStream() per accepted frame — a burst causes unbounded synchronous re-renders (UI DoS). No per-sender throttle.

Message id is fully attacker-controlled (env.id from the wire). A hostile peer can pre-publish a message with the SAME id a victim will optimistically generate, or spoof another author's id, causing the victim's real send to be silently deduped as 'duplicate' and dropped. ids should be namespaced by `from` on receive, or dedupe keyed on (from,id).

Sender identity is fully trusted from frame.from but the display `name` is attacker-supplied and unsanitized/unbounded — a peer can impersonate any display name and ship a 4000-char name (only text is length-capped, name is not). No length cap on name in parseEnvelope.

Stream error handling has no reconnect/backoff: onStreamError surfaces a banner and the consume loop sets streaming=false and exits. The user must manually click Reconnect (which calls boot() and rebuilds everything). A transient SSE drop permanently kills live updates until manual action.

newMessageId uses Math.random salt + a module-global nonceCounter shared across all ChatService instances; collision-resistant in practice but not cryptographically unique and the global counter is a shared-mutable-state hazard if two services coexist (e.g. tests, or future multi-identity).

send() adds the optimistic message and fires onMessages BEFORE awaiting publish; if publish throws, the pending message stays in the store and is only marked failed via a *separate* Set in main.ts (a.failed), which the store/service know nothing about — failed state is UI-only and lost on any rebuild/reload.
location.reload() is used as the mechanism to apply a display-name change (rebuildServiceWithName), discarding all in-memory history and presence. Heavy-handed and data-losing for a routine action.
composerValue is a module-global string shared across channels; selectChannel clears it but a re-render mid-typing or two composers could desync. No per-channel draft preservation.
DEFAULT_NODE_URL falls back to http://127.0.0.1:8844 with no configurability (no env/localStorage override) — can't point at a remote node or alternate port without editing source.

testGaps.area: main.ts (entire UI/render/boot/modal/timer layer, ~780 lines) has zero tests
sdk-adapter.ts (meshAdapter, fetchIdentity, safeUtf8) is untested — the one module touching the real SDK
dom.ts el()/clear()/linkify() untested; linkify URL-boundary and XSS-safety (no innerHTML) deserve explicit assertions
Stream reconnect/backoff behavior — there is no reconnect logic and no test asserting recovery after a transient drop
Duplicate/spoofed message id from a hostile peer (dedupe-griefing) — no test that two different senders using the same id are kept distinct
Concurrency: interleaved frames from many peers, optimistic send racing its own echo under load, ordering stability under burst
Property tests for store ordering invariants (sort stability, eviction never drops a kept message out of order) and normalizeChannelName idempotence
Two+ node end-to-end against a real ce node (only the in-memory fake is exercised; no e2e harness)
Oversize/abusive name field, deeply nested JSON, and very large payloads through parseEnvelope (DoS bounds)

docGaps: No architecture/design doc beyond README; the MeshLike port boundary and the reducer/dedupe model deserve a short ARCHITECTURE.md.
No JSDoc on the public ChatService methods describing failure modes (e.g. that send() leaves a pending message on publish failure, that join() throws on 403).
No documented wire-protocol spec/versioning policy for the envelope (PROTOCOL_VERSION exists but there's no doc on how v2 migration/compat is handled).

README claims a feature set close to Slack ('a tiny Slack') but does not enumerate what is intentionally NOT supported (threads/reactions/history) — a Limitations section is needed to set expectations.
No examples/doctests; no CONTRIBUTING; CHANGELOG absent (single 'Initial commit').
No note that custom channels/DMs are not persisted across reload, nor that presence only covers the active channel — both are surprising behaviors users will hit.

name: ce-mail
currentState: ce-mail is a genuinely well-built Rust library (edition 2024) implementing decentralized, identity-native messaging over the CE SDK, with a small CLI. The architecture is clean and layered: crypto.rs (Ed25519->X25519 NaCl-style sealed-box E2E body encryption on curve25519-dalek + ChaCha20-Poly1305), envelope.rs (tiny signed metadata + content-addressed body CID, domain-separated bincode signing), receipt.rs (signed delivery/read receipts), proto.rs (a single serde request/reply enum over ce-rs app-messaging), mailbox.rs (store-and-forward store with idempotent de-dup, bounded retention, and a ce-cap capability gate), service.rs (pure request->store state machine), thread.rs (in_reply_to + subject-normalization threading), client.rs (high-level MailClient with a Transport trait so the full send->store->drain->decrypt flow is unit-testable against an in-memory fake), and main.rs (send/inbox/read/grant-mailbox/serve-mailbox CLI). What genuinely works end-to-end: signed envelopes, E2E sealed bodies, store-and-forward with cursor drain + pagination + ack, idempotent delivery, capability-gated open-relay protection, signed receipts, and threading. What is documented-but-NOT-implemented: postage/anti-spam enforcement (the envelope field and threat-model exist but no minimum-postage check, no payment-channel wiring, no refund flow), screening/allowlists/contacts, the SMTP gateway (docs/smtp-gateway.md only), attachments (attachment_cids exists on the wire but SendOptions cannot set them and open_body never fetches them), and subject encryption. No unwrap()/expect()/panic!/todo!/unimplemented! exist in any production path — all are confined to #[cfg(test)] modules. Logging in the long-running mailbox loop uses eprintln! rather than tracing, violating project standards.
plan: Make mailbox persistence crash-safe and efficient: replace the per-request full-store std::fs::write with an atomic temp-file + rename, batch/throttle writes (or append a small WAL), and fsync. This is the highest-severity production risk (data loss for all recipients).

Implement attachments end-to-end: add attachments: Vec<Vec<u8>> (or Vec<(name, bytes)>) to SendOptions, seal+store each as a blob, populate attachment_cids, and add MailClient::open_attachment(envelope, index) that lazily fetches+decrypts. Add e2e + property tests. This closes the biggest gap vs. the claimed feature set.

Add protocol-level resource bounds: enforce a max envelope/subject/body-CID-count and a server-side max DrainPage limit; reject oversized Deliver payloads in MailService::handle before storing. Add property tests for the bounds. Closes the DoS/memory-amplification holes.

Implement postage anti-spam enforcement: define a recipient ScreeningPolicy (allowlist/contacts + require-postage-from-strangers), verify the postage_receipt as a real payment-channel receipt via ce-rs, and gate delivery/surfacing on it. Wire the refund-on-not-spam path. This realizes the threat-model's central promise.

Add screening/allowlists/contacts and an is_newcomer()/history-based prioritization using GET /history/:node_id, plus a spam vs inbox separation in the client. Test allowlist-waives-postage and stranger-requires-postage.

Refresh revocation periodically in serve-mailbox (re-fetch ce.revoked() on an interval or per-N requests) so on-chain revocation actually takes effect on long-running mailboxes; add an integration test for revoke-after-start.

Replace eprintln!/println! in serve_mailbox and library-adjacent paths with tracing per project standards; switch the mailbox loop from a 500ms poll to a streaming subscription if ce-rs exposes one, with bounded backoff on errors.

Add concurrency hardening + tests: wrap MailboxStore for concurrent access (the node serves requests concurrently) and add a multi-threaded test exercising interleaved deliver/drain/ack to prove no lost-update or double-evict.

Surface encode/serialize failures instead of unwrap_or_default(): make encode()/to_bytes() return Result (or at minimum log + propagate) so a serialization failure never silently produces an empty message/store.

Build the SMTP gateway MVP (inbound relay -> envelope, outbound CE->SMTP with address mapping) per docs/smtp-gateway.md, clearly scoped as non-E2E for legacy interop, with integration tests against a local SMTP sink.

Add CLI + CeTransport integration tests (currently only the in-memory fake is covered) and add multi-recipient/CC modeling. Update README/rustdoc to mark unimplemented features explicitly and add a runnable doctest.

featureGaps.feature: Attachments (functional, not just a wire field)
featureGaps.notes: EnvelopeBody.attachment_cids exists and is signed, but SendOptions has no attachments field, send() always sets attachment_cids: vec![], and Message/open_body never seals, stores, or fetches attachment blobs. Gmail's core attach/download is entirely unimplemented despite docs claiming lazy 40MB fetch.

postage_receipt is a pass-through opaque string; nothing validates it, requires it, locks credits, or refunds. The threat-model explicitly marks this as 'next milestone'. Without it the spam story is aspirational. Need a recipient screening policy that requires postage from strangers and a payment-channel receipt verifier.
No address book, no allow-cap, no is_newcomer()/history-based prioritization, no spam vs inbox separation, no block/mute. Gmail's filtering and categorization are absent entirely.
docs/smtp-gateway.md only; zero code. No inbound/outbound bridge, no address mapping, no SPF/DKIM handling. Gmail is fundamentally an SMTP endpoint.
Subject is cleartext metadata. No option to seal subjects for known contacts. Gmail TLS-protects everything in transit.

featureGaps.feature: Search / labels / folders / read-unread state / star/archive
featureGaps.notes: Inbox is a flat drained list saved to inbox.json; no full-text search, no labels, no folders, no per-message flags beyond cryptographic receipts. The 'read' command does prefix lookup over the last snapshot only.
EnvelopeBody.to is a single NodeId. No CC/BCC, no group/list addressing, no fan-out. Each recipient needs a separately sealed body (per-recipient ECDH) which is not modeled.

featureGaps.feature: Drafts, outbox, send-retry, scheduled send, undo send
featureGaps.notes: send() is fire-and-forget; no draft persistence, no outbox queue, no retry/backoff on transient mesh failures, no scheduled or delayed send.

Mailbox store is in-memory HashMap, persisted only by rewriting the whole file on every request in serve-mailbox. No replication, no fsync/atomic-write, no WAL; a crash mid-write can corrupt the store. Gmail is highly available and durable.

featureGaps.feature: Rich content (HTML bodies, inline images, MIME)
featureGaps.notes: Bodies are opaque bytes rendered as UTF-8 lossy text; no MIME, content-type, or HTML rendering model.

robustnessIssues.issue: Whole-store rewrite on every request with no atomic write or fsync: serve_mailbox does std::fs::write(p, svc.store().to_bytes()) after each handled message. A crash or disk-full mid-write truncates/corrupts the entire mailbox (all recipients' mail). Should write to a temp file + rename, and avoid O(n) full-serialization per message.

No byte-size bounds on envelopes, subjects, or the Deliver payload. capacity_per_recipient bounds message COUNT, not bytes. A single envelope can carry an arbitrarily large subject string (it is cleartext and signed but unbounded), and DrainPage limit is usize with no server-side max, so a malicious or buggy client/peer can request a huge page. DoS / memory-amplification vector.

Revocation set is fetched once at mailbox startup (ce.revoked()) and captured in a move closure; it never refreshes. A capability revoked after the mailbox starts is still honored until restart, defeating on-chain revocation for long-running mailboxes.

serve-mailbox uses a 500ms poll loop over ce.messages() instead of an event/stream subscription; under load this both adds latency and can drop/lag. Also messages() failure only sleeps 2s and retries with no backoff cap or circuit breaking.

Deliver does not bind the authenticated requester to envelope.body.from. This is defensible (envelopes are self-authenticating, so relays may forward), but it means there is no per-sender rate limiting or accountability at the mailbox — combined with no postage enforcement, a holder of a valid accept-grant (or the recipient's own delegate) can flood the recipient's bounded queue, silently evicting legitimate mail via capacity eviction.

encode()/to_bytes()/encode_sealed() swallow bincode errors with unwrap_or_default(), producing empty byte vectors on failure. An empty encode silently corrupts a message/store rather than surfacing an error; decode then fails downstream far from the cause.

x25519_public_from_node_id accepts any 32-byte point that decompresses; no small-subgroup / low-order point check before ECDH. With ephemeral-static sealing this is low-risk, but a hardened impl should reject low-order recipient points to avoid invalid-curve edge cases.
Inbox snapshot (inbox.json) and cursor are written non-atomically and the cursor advances even if save_snapshot partially fails; a crash can desync the saved cursor from what 'read' can see.

testGaps.area: Mailbox store-corruption / partial-write recovery in serve-mailbox
Revocation refresh while a mailbox is running (revoked-after-start grant must be rejected)
Large-payload / oversized-subject / huge DrainPage limit DoS bounds
Concurrent delivery + drain + ack against one MailboxStore (the real node will have concurrent requests; store is sync and tests are single-threaded)
Attachments end-to-end (none exist because the feature is unimplemented)
Postage enforcement / refund flow (unimplemented, untested)
CLI commands (send/inbox/read/grant-mailbox/serve-mailbox) and snapshot/cursor persistence
CeTransport real-node path (only the in-memory FakeNet/Handle is exercised; ce-rs CeClient calls are untested)
Cross-version persistence beyond the v1->v2 legacy case (forward-compat / corrupted-magic handling)
Low-order / invalid recipient point handling in seal()

docGaps: README and module docs describe attachments, postage enforcement, and the SMTP gateway as if they work; they are unimplemented. Docs should clearly mark these as not-yet-implemented to avoid overclaiming (the threat-model is honest about postage, but lib.rs/envelope.rs read as if attachments are functional).
No rustdoc doctests are runnable: the lib.rs example is #[no_run] and uses a placeholder recipient id; there are no compile-tested examples of the receipt or threading APIs.

No architecture/sequence doc for the mailbox request loop, persistence model, or the capability-gate authorization derivation (the mailbox.rs module comment is good but there is no top-level ARCHITECTURE.md or diagram).
No documentation of operational limits (max body/subject size, capacity semantics = count not bytes, poll interval, persistence durability guarantees).
CLI help is present but there is no usage walkthrough in README for the full offline flow (grant-mailbox -> serve-mailbox -> send --mailbox -> inbox --ack).
